verdict: ttfs (analytical) is LOSSLESS on the fixed mlp_mixer/MNIST/S=4 vehicle: deployed 0.9807 +/- 0.49pp (n=3) vs ANN 0.9821, gap +0.15pp = inside the seed sd. Best deployed of the family. Caveat: this is the analytical mode that propagates real-valued membrane V across cores (NOT binary spiking), so a near-zero gap is the expected behavior of the least-lossy instrument; it is not evidence about genuine spike fidelity. Per-seed 0.975/0.983/0.984. Deployed read from nevresim Simulation step.

ttfs_quantized is EFFECTIVELY LOSSLESS on the fixed vehicle: deployed 0.9773 +/- 0.45pp vs ANN 0.9829, gap +0.55pp (~1.2x seed sd) -- a real sub-1pp gap, best among the genuinely act-quantized modes, and the fastest of the lossless pair (~500s). It is the only mode here with activation_quantization=on + encoding_layer_placement=offload (that rides along with the mode choice). Still analytical-V, not binary-spiking. Per-seed 0.977/0.973/0.982.

ttfs_cycle synchronized is NOT lossless on this vehicle: deployed 0.9607 +/- 0.25pp vs ANN 0.9826, gap +2.19pp (well outside seed sd), but it is the TIGHTEST mode (sd 0.25pp) and beats its cascaded sibling by +0.84pp on the only differing knob (ttfs_cycle_schedule). INSTRUMENT CAVEAT: sync deployed number comes from the Hard Core Mapping (SCM/HCM analytical) step, NOT a completed nevresim Simulation step (enable_nevresim_simulation=true but no Simulation step ran). Per the fidelity lock, synchronized ttfs_cycle is SCM<->HCM psum-bit-exact (0.0 float64), so this is a faithful proxy but a DIFFERENT instrument from the other 4 modes. Per-seed 0.9582/0.9608/0.9632.

lif is NOT lossless on this vehicle: deployed 0.9600 +/- 0.70pp vs ANN 0.9829, gap +2.29pp. This is a BUDGET statement not a capacity one -- the LIF memory line shows LIF reaches full losslessness (>= ANN) only when stabilize steps are pushed 400->6000, which this S=4 fixed-budget config does not do. Genuine binary-spike rate code. Slowest mode (~1980s wall). Per-seed 0.960/0.953/0.967 (widest non-cascaded spread). Deployed read from nevresim Simulation step.

ttfs_cycle cascaded is the LOSSY OUTLIER -- last in the family by every measure: deployed 0.9523 +/- 0.91pp vs ANN 0.9834, gap +3.11pp (largest gap, widest seed spread). It is the only genuine single-spike ramp-reconstructed binary-spike schedule and pays for that genuineness: -0.84pp vs its synchronized sibling (clean, the only differing knob is ttfs_cycle_schedule), -2.5 to -2.9pp vs the analytical TTFS modes. Highest variance is consistent with cold-cascade death-cascade fragility. Shallow-depth corroboration of the WS3 depth law (cascaded < synchronized at every trainable depth). Per-seed 0.942/0.959/0.956. Deployed read from nevresim Simulation step.

WS-mode per-mode landscape (mlp_mixer_core / MNIST / S=4 / n=3, same vehicle, 15/15 rc0). verdict: The keystone (conversion_policy:true) RESCUES the cascaded d8 deficit 0.8754->0.9452 (+7pp) end-to-end. Mechanism: propose_recipe ALWAYS proposes driver=controller (conversion_policy.py:225), so an enabled decision -- MATCH or ESCALATE -- sets axis=controller (ttfs_adaptation_plan.py EF3), vetoing the fast 5-rung ladder and routing to the controller's 8-rung gradual ramp + adaptive post-finalize recovery. The recovery (finalize_cliff 0.45 -> ~0.94) is what closes ~70pp of the genuine-cascade cliff. NOTE: this CONFIRMS the keystone changes the driver and recovers accuracy, but the ESCALATE-vs-MATCH branch is NOT distinguishable from the log alone (both yield driver=controller); the no-blend control (next record) proves the rescue is the CONTROLLER, not the escalation per se.

evidence: With ttfs_blend_fast:false the fast ladder is OFF in BOTH arms, so BOTH run the 8-rung controller ladder. cpFalse (no keystone) deploys 0.9396 and cpTrue (keystone) 0.9417 -- statistically indistinguishable (+0.21pp, within seed sd ~0.3pp). cp_d8 control (blend ON): cpFalse 0.8782 (fast, 5-rung) vs cpTrue 0.9418 (controller, 8-rung) = +6.4pp.
verdict: ABLATION ISOLATES THE LEVER: the ~7pp d8 rescue is the CONTROLLER driver (gradual ramp + adaptive post-finalize recovery), NOT the conversion-policy keystone itself. When the fast ladder is removed (blend_fast:false), cpFalse==cpTrue (~0.94) -- the controller alone reaches the rescued accuracy without the policy. The keystone's ONLY effect is to SELECT the controller (propose_recipe -> driver=controller) and thereby veto the fast ladder; it adds no accuracy beyond what driver=controller already delivers. So 'the escalation path works AND recovers accuracy' is TRUE only in the weak sense that the keystone correctly routes to the controller; the recovery is the controller's, and ESCALATE is not separable from MATCH on this cell (both -> controller).
